what: What we set out to do
title: NA
text: goal session take existing personal project — data-driven CV built Quarto Typst — turn bonafide R package called curriculr. original project working self-contained workflow: rendered CV Excel workbook tightly coupled personal data, file paths, design choices. package version needed generalize anyone use . also used session make explicit set design decisions implicit original code, establish conventions carry rest package development.

what: Background: what the original project looked like
title: NA
text: original workflow consisted three files: CV.qmd main Quarto document. sourced two R helper scripts, read Excel workbook, emitted raw Typst blocks CV section. document rendered PDF using Quarto’s Typst engine — LaTeX, vitae, custom .cls files. part already significant improvement original R Markdown version. Excel workbook (data_driven_cv_data.xlsx) held CV content organized across eleven sheets: admin_services, affiliations, certifications, education, experience, grants_and_awards, invited_teaching, presentations, projects, publications, skills. R helper scripts provided: read_cv_data() — read sheets named list data frames family Typst-building functions: typst_escape(), cv_value(), cv_date_range(), cv_year_range(), cv_section(), cv_entry(), emit_entries(), emit_simple_entries() code well-written well-documented, two problems needed solving become package: contained hardcoded personal information, schema inconsistency across sheets made rendering functions complex necessary.
code: CV.qmd R/read-cv-data.R R/typst-cv-functions.R

title: NA
text: confirmed name curriculr. search CRAN GitHub found existing package name. name play curriculum vitae R community’s convention dropping trailing vowels. Branding assets — hex sticker favicon — already created placed man/figures/.

title: NA
text: curriculr standalone package, part toolero. depend toolero runtime one specific purpose: create_cv() delegate toolero’s create_qmd() Quarto scaffolding step. makes toolero curriculr siblings — curriculr reuses one primitive toolero without conceptually merged . runtime dependencies : toolero, readxl, cli, fs, glue, purrr. Development tools (devtools, usethis, roxygen2, testthat) tracked renv appear DESCRIPTION. license MIT, matching toolero. Apache 2 (used containr) considered rejected — patent protections adds MIT meaningful CV-rendering package.

what: Schema changes: normalizing the Excel workbook
title: NA
text: original workbook three columns used section-specific names conceptually thing — primary entry label: skills sheet also renamed skills → unit match secondary text convention used everywhere else. motivation cv_render_section() — successor emit_entries() — able treat every sheet identically. section-specific column names forced special-case handling CV.qmd made rendering functions harder reason . title normalized across sheets, caller simply passes title_col = "title" every time. affiliations sheet single column renamed unit title reason.

title: NA
text: original CV.qmd personal information hardcoded directly raw Typst block: important thing fix. package ship one person’s name address baked template. solution add profile sheet workbook single source truth personal information. sheet uses simple two-column schema: dividing line belongs workbook belongs YAML : everything answers person lives spreadsheet; everything answers render (paper size, margins, params) lives YAML front matter. read_cv_data() handles profile sheet specially — converts two-column data frame named character vector template code can write cv$profile[["first_name"]] rather clunky row-filter expression.

title: NA
text: readme sheet added workbooks — personal workbook Frank Palmer example workbook — make schema self-documenting. sheet explains: general rules data entry column--column reference table date entry quick-reference covering combinations ongoing, completed, point--time, undated entries profile sheet field guide sheet generated programmatically using openpyxl Python, UW red header rows, alternating row fills, wrapped text, frozen top rows. top three rows (title, intro paragraph, spacer) frozen remain visible scrolling reference tables.

title: NA
text: original code two iterator functions: emit_entries() — sections dates, locations, detail emit_simple_entries() — sections without dates locations distinction false. “simple” entry just regular entry fewer fields populated — different kind thing. two functions implied meaningful difference exist. collapsed single function: cv_render_section(). Optional columns default NULL simply omitted rendered output. date_fun defaults .cv_date_range() dated sections passed NULL undated sections like skills affiliations. caller controls simplicity arguments, function call.

title: NA
text: original code, emit_entries() constructed Typst strings called cat() write document. made functions untestable isolation — check output without capturing stdout. package version separates two responsibilities: .build_section_blocks() — internal builder, iterates rows, returns character vector Typst blocks, one per row. Fully testable. cv_render_section() — thin public wrapper, calls .build_section_blocks() passes result cat(). function lives inside Quarto chunk results = 'asis'. results = 'asis' chunk option tells Quarto pass R output raw document without formatting wrapper. cat() writes directly output stream. builder knows nothing cat() — just returns strings.

title: NA
text: Two conventions established applied consistently across source files: Function names: internal functions carry . prefix. Exported functions . File names: source files use kebab-case.R. Function names inside files use snake_case. matches convention already established toolero containr.

what: Decision: sprintf() over glue::glue() for Typst strings
title: NA
text: Typst layout strings contain many literal { } characters, glue’s interpolation delimiters. Using glue require escaping every literal brace {{ }}, making strings harder read maintain. sprintf() uses %s placeholders conflict Typst syntax , kept layout functions. glue remains available parts package — particularly create_cv() — genuinely readable.

what: Decision: create_cv() architecture — Model A abandoned, self-contained instead
title: NA
text: Two models considered create_cv() relate create_qmd() toolero: Model : create_cv() calls create_qmd() internally, passing CV-specific template YAML structure. Model B: extract shared internal helper functions call. Model initially chosen. However, examined toolero’s actual create_qmd() function signature: became clear create_qmd() accept template argument always uses internal template. delegation model implementable without toolero release. create_cv() therefore written self-contained function handles scaffolding steps directly — workbook copy, placeholder image copy, Quarto template copy — without calling toolero . toolero dependency removed DESCRIPTION. Delegation can revisited toolero v0.4.0 adds template argument create_qmd().
code: create_qmd(filename, path, yaml_data, overwrite, use_purl)

title: NA
text: original CV.qmd three problems needed solving shipped package template: Personal information hardcoded raw Typst block Helper scripts loaded via source() rather via package Column names old schema (degree, accomplishment, area) generalized template addresses three. Personal information now comes cv$profile. raw Typst header block converted R chunk results: asis. chunk reads profile field named character vector returned read_cv_data(), escapes every value .typst_escape(), assembles contact line dynamically — omitting blank fields automatically — writes Typst block via sprintf(). personal data appears anywhere template file . Helper scripts gone. template now loads curriculr library(curriculr) calls read_cv_data() directly. two source() calls original replaced one library() call. Column names updated throughout reflect normalized schema: title everywhere, institution education organization line. emit_entries() emit_simple_entries() replaced cv_render_section() every section block. document style settings block — font, size, color palette — remains raw {=typst} block. rendering concerns, data concerns, belong template rather workbook.

what: The problem with maintaining a CV
title: Why curriculr: The Case for a Data-Driven CV
text: people maintain CV way: open Word document PDF, find right section, type new line, fix formatting broke, adjust spacing, export, hope looks right. six months later need different version different audience. approach name software development: called manual data entry presentation layer, exactly kind work software exists eliminate. fundamental problem CV mixes two things kept separate: content (done, , published) presentation (fonts, margins, section order, color). bundled together single Word document, changing one risks breaking . Updating job title can knock section onto wrong page. Changing font size fit content can cascade entire document. curriculr separates .

title: Why curriculr: The Case for a Data-Driven CV
text: curriculr, content lives Excel workbook. sheet corresponds one section CV. get new publication, add row publications sheet. give talk, add row presentations. never touch layout. Presentation lives CV.qmd – Quarto document reads workbook renders PDF using Typst. document handles fonts, margins, colors, section headings, date formatting, two-column entry layout. touch unless want change visual design. pipeline : want updated PDF, call: entire workflow.
code: Excel workbook  ->  R (curriculr)  ->  Quarto + Typst  ->  CV.pdf create_cv(   data  = "cv-data.xlsx",   photo = "me.jpeg" )

previous_headings: How curriculr compares to other approaches
title: Why curriculr: The Case for a Data-Driven CV
text: common approach. content formatting inseparable, makes consistent updates difficult. Producing multiple versions different audiences – full CV, short resume, teaching-focused version – means maintaining multiple documents quickly drift sync. curriculr keeps one source truth. Different versions supported future release via parameters filter sections entries render time.

previous_headings: How curriculr compares to other approaches
title: Why curriculr: The Case for a Data-Driven CV
text: LaTeX separates content presentation better Word, produces beautiful PDFs. barrier real: LaTeX requires working installation, knowledge language, tolerance cryptic error messages. Custom CV templates like Awesome CV add layer complexity .cls files .sty dependencies. curriculr uses Typst instead LaTeX. Typst faster, cleaner error messages, requires installation beyond Quarto. Typst layout CV.qmd transparent R-adjacent code rather black-box class file.

previous_headings: How curriculr compares to other approaches
title: Why curriculr: The Case for a Data-Driven CV
text: vitae R package closest prior art curriculr deserves direct acknowledgment – curriculr inspired . vitae provides family CV templates built R Markdown, including Awesome CV template several others. handles rendering pipeline provides helper functions like detailed_entries() map data frames CV entries. curriculr differs: curriculr uses Quarto Typst rather R Markdown LaTeX. removes LaTeX dependency entirely produces faster renders cleaner error messages. curriculr uses Excel data source rather R data frames CSV files. many researchers, spreadsheet natural editing environment code. curriculr puts section control workbook. Adding, removing, reordering sections requires R code changes – editing sections sheet. curriculr single-function workflow. create_cv() handles scaffolding, template injection, rendering one call.

previous_headings: How curriculr compares to other approaches
title: Why curriculr: The Case for a Data-Driven CV
text: Nick Strayer’s datadrivencv package shares curriculr’s philosophy treating CV structured data. uses Google Sheets data source pagedown rendering. remains influential design worth examining anyone interested data-driven CV space. curriculr’s differences: local Excel instead Google Sheets (authentication required, works offline), Typst instead pagedown (PDF-first rather HTML-first), active maintenance.

title: Why curriculr: The Case for a Data-Driven CV
text: Using Excel data source deliberate choice R users find surprising. reasons works well practice: already data lives. academics professionals already track accomplishments kind spreadsheet. Asking maintain CV data tool already use lowers barrier keeping current. accessible collaborators. department administrator helping maintain faculty member’s CV can edit Excel file without knowing R. schema documented workbook’s readme sheet. supports structured data naturally. sheet table. Dates dates. Text text. schema explicit consistent. Compared editing Word document, adding new publication spreadsheet row precise, reversible, auditable operation. workbook documentation. readme sheet explains column schema, date conventions, sections control system. new user can open workbook understand without reading package documentation.

title: Why curriculr: The Case for a Data-Driven CV
text: curriculr CV reproducible sense R analysis script reproducible: given workbook version package, PDF produced every time. workbook can version-controlled alongside rendered output. Changes CV content visible workbook’s edit history. entire history career can preserved single Excel file rendered demand. matters researchers care reproducibility work. consistent apply principles CV.

title: Why curriculr: The Case for a Data-Driven CV
text: result CV.pdf directory workbook.
code: # Install pak::pak("erwinlares/curriculr")  # Scaffold -- copies template workbook and placeholder photo to getwd() library(curriculr) create_cv()  # Edit cv-data-template.xlsx, then render create_cv(   data  = "cv-data-template.xlsx",   photo = "your-photo.png" )

title: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst
text: Call create_cv() arguments. copies template workbook placeholder profile image current working directory: see:
code: library(curriculr) create_cv() v Created cv-data-template.xlsx v Created placeholder.png i Next steps: 1. Open cv-data-template.xlsx and fill in the profile sheet. 2. Replace placeholder.png with your own profile photo. 3. Call create_cv(data = 'cv-data-template.xlsx', photo = 'your-photo.png')

title: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst
text: Open cv-data-template.xlsx. Start profile sheet: fill remaining sheets CV content. readme sheet inside workbook explains column schema date entry conventions detail.

title: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst
text: writes CV.qmd CV.pdf next workbook. Open CV.pdf review output. update CV, edit workbook call create_cv() overwrite = TRUE. render without profile photo, omit photo argument. header use single-column layout name, contact line, profile statement centered page:
code: create_cv(   data  = "cv-data-template.xlsx",   photo = "your-photo.png" ) create_cv(data = "cv-data-template.xlsx")

what: For existing users updating from v0.2.0
title: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst
text: v0.3.0 introduces several workbook schema changes new arguments. need know. photo = NULL now means photo. Previously, omitting photo render mode fell back bundled placeholder image. now produces single-column header layout image. Supply path explicitly restore two-column layout. workbook needs include_in_resume column every section sheet. boolean column (checked/unchecked) controls rows appear rendering resume variant mode. Add column named include_in_resume section sheet check rows want include focused resume. Rows without column always included. workbook can optional theme sheet. sheet controls fonts, colors, page layout. absent, built-defaults used. Copy theme sheet updated template workbook gain full control visual appearance without editing CV.qmd directly. readxl longer required. curriculr now uses openxlsx2 workbook reading. readxl explicit dependencies curriculr-related code, can remove .

what: For existing users updating from v0.1.0
title: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst
text: v0.2.0 changes create_cv() works adds sections sheet workbook schema. need know. create_cv() now two modes: Scaffold mode (arguments) — copies template files current directory. Use get started reset. Render mode (arguments) — reads workbook renders PDF. Use every time update CV. workbook needs sections sheet. sheet controls sections appear rendered PDF order. Add manually copy updated template workbook. schema : Row order render order. Delete row exclude section. Add row sheet follows standard column schema render automatically without R code changes. date_fun column accepts five tokens:

title: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst
text: want shorter, focused version CV, use variant = "resume". curriculr include rows checked include_in_resume column section sheet: Check uncheck rows directly workbook. R code changes needed switch CV resume variants.
code: create_cv(   data        = "cv-data-template.xlsx",   photo       = "your-photo.png",   variant     = "resume",   output_file = "resume.pdf" )

what: Changing colors, fonts, and page layout

title: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst

title: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst
text: Use add_section() add new sheet register sections control sheet one step: new sheet pre-populated standard column spine. Open workbook, add data, re-render.
code: add_section(   "cv-data-template.xlsx",   section  = "patents",   label    = "Patents",   date_fun = "year_only" )

title: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst
text: main entry point. arguments triggers scaffold mode. data triggers render mode. Arguments: data — path workbook. NULL triggers scaffold mode. photo — path profile image. NULL renders without photo. output_file — PDF filename. Defaults "CV.pdf". overwrite — whether overwrite existing files. Defaults FALSE. variant — "cv" (rows) "resume" (checked rows ). use_icons — "fontawesome" (default) "none".
code: # Scaffold mode create_cv()  # Render mode — full CV with Font Awesome icons in the contact line create_cv(   data        = "~/my_cv/cv-data.xlsx",   photo       = "~/my_cv/me.jpeg",   output_file = "erwin-lares-cv.pdf" )  # Render mode — resume variant, plain text contact line create_cv(   data        = "~/my_cv/cv-data.xlsx",   photo       = "~/my_cv/me.jpeg",   variant     = "resume",   use_icons   = "none",   output_file = "erwin-lares-resume.pdf" )

title: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst
text: Adds new sheet existing workbook registers sections control sheet.
code: add_section(   "cv-data-template.xlsx",   section   = "invited_talks",   label     = "Invited Talks",   date_fun  = "month_year",   where_col = "where" )

title: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst
text: Reads sheets workbook named list. profile theme sheets become named character vectors; section sheets become data frames; sections sheet returned row order.
code: cv <- read_cv_data("cv-data-template.xlsx") cv <- read_cv_data("cv-data-template.xlsx", variant = "resume")  cv$experience           # a data frame cv$sections             # the sections control sheet cv$profile[["email"]]  # a scalar string cv$theme[["accent_color"]]  # a scalar string

title: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst

code: title | unit | startMonth | startYear | endMonth | endYear | where | detail | include_in_resume

what: Add a new section to a curriculr workbook — add_section
title: Add a new section to a curriculr workbook — add_section
text: Adds new sheet existing curriculr-formatted Excel workbook registers sections control sheet. new sheet pre-populated standard column spine user can start entering data immediately without worrying column names.

title: Add a new section to a curriculr workbook — add_section
code: add_section(   workbook,   section,   label = section,   date_fun = "year_only",   title_col = "title",   org_col = "unit",   detail_col = NA,   where_col = "where",   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Add a new section to a curriculr workbook — add_section
text: workbook character string. Path existing curriculr Excel workbook. section character string. Internal name new section. Must valid Excel sheet name (31 characters, special characters). name must match sheet name exactly referenced elsewhere workbook cap arguments create_cv(). label character string. Display label shown section heading rendered CV. Defaults section, works section name already human-readable. Supply different value internal name display label differ, e.g. section = "invited_talks", label = "Invited Talks". date_fun character string. Token controlling date formatting section. One "date", "year", "month_year", "year_only", "none". Defaults "year_only". See resolve_date_fun() token definitions. title_col character string. Name column used primary entry label rendered CV. Defaults "title". org_col character string NA. Name column used secondary organization venue line. Defaults "unit". Pass NA omit organization line section. detail_col character string NA. Name column used detail line. Defaults NA (omitted). Pass column name include detail line. where_col character string NA. Name column used location. Defaults "". Pass NA omit location section. overwrite logical. Whether overwrite existing sheet name. Defaults FALSE. FALSE, existing sheet causes informative error. destructive operation — use care.

title: Add a new section to a curriculr workbook — add_section
text: Invisibly returns workbook. Called primarily side effect modifying workbook disk.

title: Add a new section to a curriculr workbook — add_section
text: add_section() performs following steps: Validates workbook exists section already present (unless overwrite = TRUE). Appends new sheet named section standard column spine: title | unit | startMonth | startYear | endMonth | endYear | | detail | include_in_resume. Appends new row sections sheet registering new section supplied metadata. overwrite = TRUE, existing row section replaced. Writes modified workbook back workbook place. workbook modified place. undo. Consider keeping backup copy calling add_section() workbook contains data reconstruct. Control sheets (profile, sections, theme, readme) used section names.

title: Add a new section to a curriculr workbook — add_section
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Add a section with defaults add_section("cv-data.xlsx", section = "patents")  # Add a section with a display label that differs from the sheet name add_section("cv-data.xlsx",             section  = "invited_talks",             label    = "Invited Talks",             date_fun = "month_year")  # Add a section without an organization or location line add_section("cv-data.xlsx",             section   = "languages",             label     = "Languages",             date_fun  = "none",             org_col   = NA,             where_col = NA) } # }

what: Generate a CV document from a curriculr workbook — create_cv
title: Generate a CV document from a curriculr workbook — create_cv
text: Called arguments, create_cv() runs scaffold mode: copies template Excel workbook placeholder profile image current working directory prints instructions next step. rendering takes place.

title: Generate a CV document from a curriculr workbook — create_cv
code: create_cv(   data = NULL,   photo = NULL,   output_file = "CV.pdf",   overwrite = FALSE,   variant = "cv",   use_icons = "fontawesome" )

title: Generate a CV document from a curriculr workbook — create_cv
text: data character string NULL. Path Excel workbook. Defaults NULL, triggers scaffold mode. photo character string NULL. Path profile image. Defaults NULL, renders CV without profile photo using single-column header layout. Supply path use photo two-column header layout. output_file character string. Name output PDF file. Defaults "CV.pdf". Ignored scaffold mode. overwrite logical. Whether overwrite existing files. Defaults FALSE. variant character string. Controls content scope. "cv" (default) renders rows every section. "resume" renders rows include_in_resume checked workbook. use_icons character string. "fontawesome" (default) renders contact fields CV header Font Awesome icons via Typst @preview/fontawesome package. "none" renders plain text.

title: Generate a CV document from a curriculr workbook — create_cv
text: scaffold mode, invisibly returns path directory files copied. render mode, invisibly returns path rendered PDF.

title: Generate a CV document from a curriculr workbook — create_cv
text: Called data photo arguments, create_cv() runs render mode: reads workbook, generates CV.qmd, renders PDF using Quarto's Typst engine. CV.qmd CV.pdf written directory workbook. Scaffold mode (arguments): Copies cv-data-template.xlsx getwd(). Copies placeholder.png getwd(). Prints instructions editing workbook rendering CV. Render mode (data supplied): Resolves validates workbook photo paths. Reads workbook read_cv_data(), applying variant filtering. Resolves theme values workbook built-defaults. Writes CV.qmd injecting resolved values package template via sentinel substitution. Calls quarto::quarto_render() produce PDF. photo = NULL, CV header renders single full-width column containing name, contact line, address, profile statement. photo path supplied, header uses two-column layout photo left. variant = "resume", row-level filtering controlled entirely include_in_resume column section sheet. Check rows want included resume leave rest unchecked. Theme values (fonts, colors, page layout) read theme sheet workbook. theme sheet absent, built-defaults used. Individual keys missing partial theme sheet filled defaults.

title: Generate a CV document from a curriculr workbook — create_cv
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Scaffold mode — copy template files to current directory create_cv()  # Render mode — full CV with photo and Font Awesome icons create_cv(   data  = "~/my_cv/cv-data.xlsx",   photo = "~/my_cv/me.jpeg" )  # Render mode — no photo, single-column header create_cv(   data = "~/my_cv/cv-data.xlsx" )  # Render mode — resume variant create_cv(   data        = "~/my_cv/cv-data.xlsx",   photo       = "~/my_cv/me.jpeg",   variant     = "resume",   output_file = "resume.pdf" )  # Render mode — plain text contact line, custom output filename create_cv(   data        = "~/my_cv/cv-data.xlsx",   photo       = "~/my_cv/me.jpeg",   use_icons   = "none",   output_file = "erwin-lares-cv.pdf" ) } # }

what: curriculr: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst — curriculr-package
title: curriculr: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst — curriculr-package
text: Provides tools producing data-driven curriculum vitae documents structured data stored Excel workbook. core workflow reads CV content workbook, converts Typst layout blocks, renders polished PDF via Quarto's Typst engine. Includes functions reading cleaning CV data, building Typst section headings entries, rendering CV sections data frames, scaffolding new CV projects standard folder structure template workbook. Designed separate content layout: CV data lives spreadsheet, rendering configuration lives Quarto, transformation logic lives small composable R functions. See Lauritzen (2023) https://typst.app Typst typesetting system Allaire et al. (2024) https://quarto.org Quarto publishing system. Inspired vitae package (O'Hara-Wild et al., 2024, https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=vitae) Awesome CV LaTeX template (Park, 2015, https://github.com/posquit0/Awesome-CV).

title: curriculr: Data-Driven CVs with Quarto and Typst — curriculr-package
text: Maintainer: Erwin Lares erwin.lares@wisc.edu (ORCID)

what: Build the contact line for the CV header — cv_contact_line
title: Build the contact line for the CV header — cv_contact_line
text: Assembles Typst-formatted contact line profile vector. use_icons = "fontawesome", known contact fields rendered Font Awesome icon via Typst @preview/fontawesome package. Fields icon equivalent fall back plain text warning. use_icons = "none", fields render plain text.

title: Build the contact line for the CV header — cv_contact_line
code: cv_contact_line(profile, use_icons = "fontawesome")

title: Build the contact line for the CV header — cv_contact_line
text: profile named character vector returned profile element read_cv_data(). use_icons character string. "fontawesome" (default) renders contact fields Font Awesome icons. "none" renders plain text.

title: Build the contact line for the CV header — cv_contact_line
text: character string raw Typst markup contact line.

title: Build the contact line for the CV header — cv_contact_line
text: function called inside CV.qmd exported users building custom Quarto templates can call directly.

what: Render a CV section from a data frame — cv_render_section
title: Render a CV section from a data frame — cv_render_section
text: Iterates CV data frame writes row Typst CV entry calling .cv_entry() row passing result base::cat().

title: Render a CV section from a data frame — cv_render_section
code: cv_render_section(   data,   title_col,   org_col = NULL,   detail_col = NULL,   date_fun = .cv_date_range,   where_col = "where" )

title: Render a CV section from a data frame — cv_render_section
text: data data frame containing CV entries. Typically one element list returned read_cv_data(), e.g. cv$experience. title_col character string. Name column use entry title. Required. org_col character string NULL. Name column use organization secondary text. Defaults NULL. detail_col character string NULL. Name column use additional detail. Defaults NULL. date_fun function NULL. Called row produce date string. Defaults .cv_date_range(). Pass NULL sections without dates. where_col character string NULL. Name column use location. Defaults "". Pass NULL omit location.

title: Render a CV section from a data frame — cv_render_section
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect writing Typst blocks Quarto document output stream.

title: Render a CV section from a data frame — cv_render_section
text: function intended called inside Quarto document chunk results = 'asis'. cat() call writes raw Typst blocks directly document output stream. Nothing returned — function called entirely side effect. sections use dates, pass one .cv_date_range() .cv_year_range() date_fun. sections dates relevant (skills, affiliations), pass date_fun = NULL.

title: Render a CV section from a data frame — cv_render_section
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Inside a Quarto chunk with results = 'asis':  # Full entry with date range and location cat(.cv_section("Experience")) cv_render_section(cv$experience,                   title_col  = "title",                   org_col    = "unit",                   detail_col = "detail")  # Year-only dates cat(.cv_section("Education")) cv_render_section(cv$education,                   title_col  = "title",                   org_col    = "institution",                   detail_col = "detail",                   date_fun   = .cv_year_range)  # No dates, no location cat(.cv_section("Skills")) cv_render_section(cv$skills,                   title_col = "title",                   org_col   = "unit",                   date_fun  = NULL,                   where_col = NULL)  # Custom inline date function cat(.cv_section("Presentations")) cv_render_section(cv$presentations,                   title_col = "unit",                   org_col   = "title",                   date_fun  = function(row) {                     trimws(paste(.cv_value(row, "startMonth"),                                  .cv_value(row, "startYear")))                   }) } # }

Required. org_col character string NULL. Name column use organization secondary text. Defaults NULL. detail_col character string NULL. Name column use additional detail. Defaults NULL. date_fun function NULL. Called row produce date string. Defaults .cv_date_range(). Pass NULL sections without dates. where_col character string NULL. Name column use location. Defaults "". Pass NULL omit location.

what: Format a CV date range from month and year columns — .cv_date_range
title: Format a CV date range from month and year columns — .cv_date_range
text: Builds human-readable date range CV data row using columns startMonth, startYear, endMonth, endYear. Supports ongoing entries treating endMonth value "present" (case-insensitive) "Present".

title: Format a CV date range from month and year columns — .cv_date_range
code: .cv_date_range(row)

title: Format a CV date range from month and year columns — .cv_date_range
text: row one-row data frame representing one CV entry.

title: Format a CV date range from month and year columns — .cv_date_range
text: character string containing formatted date range.

title: Create a Typst CV entry — .cv_entry
text: Generates raw Typst block one CV entry. entry laid two-column grid: left column holds title metadata; right column holds date location, right-aligned accent color.

title: Create a Typst CV entry — .cv_entry
code: .cv_entry(title = "", organization = "", detail = "", when = "", where = "")

title: Create a Typst CV entry — .cv_entry
text: title character string. main entry label: degree, job title, project name, publication title, skill area, etc. organization character string. secondary line: employer, institution, publisher, venue. Defaults "". detail character string. Additional context shown organization line. Defaults "". character string. date date range, typically produced .cv_date_range() .cv_year_range(). Defaults "". character string. Location associated entry. Defaults "".

title: Create a Typst CV entry — .cv_entry
text: character string raw Typst markup.

title: Create a Typst CV entry — .cv_entry
text: arguments optional except title. Empty strings handled gracefully — missing metadata, dates, locations simply omitted rendered output rather leaving blank space.

title: Resolve theme values, filling gaps with defaults — .resolve_theme

what: Read CV data from an Excel workbook — read_cv_data
title: Read CV data from an Excel workbook — read_cv_data
text: Reads sheets curriculr-formatted Excel workbook returns named list data frames. sheet becomes one list element, named sheet. profile sheet returned named character vector convenient scalar access. theme sheet returned named character vector keyed key column. sections sheet returned data frame order rows appear workbook — row order controls section render order must sorted.

title: Read CV data from an Excel workbook — read_cv_data
code: read_cv_data(path = "data/cv-data.xlsx", variant = "cv")

title: Read CV data from an Excel workbook — read_cv_data
text: path character string. Path Excel workbook. Defaults "data/cv-data.xlsx". variant character string. Controls rows included section sheet. "cv" (default) returns rows. "resume" returns rows include_in_resume TRUE. Sections lack include_in_resume column included full regardless variant.

title: Read CV data from an Excel workbook — read_cv_data
text: named list one element per sheet workbook. profile element named character vector; theme element named character vector (NULL theme sheet absent); elements data frames. include_in_resume column dropped returned data frames — used filtering passed rendering pipeline. Access sections cv$education, cv$experience, etc. Access profile fields cv$profile[["first_name"]]. Access theme values cv$theme[["accent_color"]]. Access sections control sheet cv$sections.

title: Read CV data from an Excel workbook — read_cv_data
text: Sheets containing startYear column sorted descending order startYear recent entries appear first. profile, theme, sections sheets exempt sorting. workbook must follow curriculr schema. Every section sheet contain title column primary entry label. profile sheet must contain field value columns. sections sheet must contain minimum section label columns. theme sheet, present, must contain key value columns. cell values read character strings import. Numeric columns startYear endYear coerced character downstream rendering treats uniformly. include_in_resume column read logical coercion used row filtering variant = "resume". Empty cells cells containing literal string "NA" converted NA. theme sheet absent, cv$theme NULL create_cv() fall back built-defaults.

title: Read CV data from an Excel workbook — read_cv_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Full CV — all rows from every section cv <- read_cv_data("data/cv-data.xlsx")  # Resume variant — only rows marked include_in_resume cv <- read_cv_data("data/cv-data.xlsx", variant = "resume")  # Access a section cv$education cv$experience  # Access the sections control sheet cv$sections  # Access profile fields cv$profile[["first_name"]] cv$profile[["email"]]  # Access theme values cv$theme[["accent_color"]] cv$theme[["papersize"]] } # }

what: Resolve a date_fun token to a function — resolve_date_fun
title: Resolve a date_fun token to a function — resolve_date_fun
text: Maps string token sections sheet corresponding date formatting function used cv_render_section(). allows date formatting behaviour controlled Excel workbook rather hardcoded Quarto template.

title: Resolve a date_fun token to a function — resolve_date_fun
code: resolve_date_fun(token)

title: Resolve a date_fun token to a function — resolve_date_fun
text: token character string. One "date", "year", "month_year", "year_only", "none".

title: Resolve a date_fun token to a function — resolve_date_fun
text: function suitable passing date_fun argument cv_render_section(), NULL token "none".

what: Escape text for safe use in Typst markup — typst_escape
title: Escape text for safe use in Typst markup — typst_escape
text: Converts input value Typst-safe character string. Removes simple HTML line break tags, collapses repeated whitespace, escapes Typst special characters, trims leading trailing whitespace.

title: Escape text for safe use in Typst markup — typst_escape
code: typst_escape(x)

title: Escape text for safe use in Typst markup — typst_escape
text: x value vector escape.

title: Escape text for safe use in Typst markup — typst_escape
text: character vector Typst-sensitive characters escaped.

title: Escape text for safe use in Typst markup — typst_escape
text: CV content comes Excel may contain characters Typst treats markup: #, $, %, &, ~, _, ^, {, }, [, ], @. @ character included Typst may interpret email addresses references labels.

title: curriculr 0.3.0
text: read_cv_data() now requires openxlsx2 package instead readxl. Remove readxl add openxlsx2 explicit dependencies projects call read_cv_data() directly. create_cv() longer falls back bundled placeholder image photo = NULL render mode. Omitting photo now produces single-column header layout image. Supply path explicitly restore two-column layout profile photo.

title: curriculr 0.3.0
text: Resume variant. create_cv() read_cv_data() gain variant argument ("cv" "resume"). variant = "resume", rows include_in_resume checked workbook included rendered output. Every section sheet now carries include_in_resume column row-level control. Font Awesome icons. create_cv() gains use_icons argument ("fontawesome" "none"). "fontawesome" (default), contact fields CV header rendered Font Awesome icons via Typst @preview/fontawesome package. Fields icon equivalent fall back plain text warning. new exported function cv_contact_line() assembles contact line can called directly custom templates. Workbook-controlled theming. new theme sheet workbook controls fonts, colors, page layout. Keys map directly Typst style block Quarto format: typst: YAML block, now injected create_cv() via sentinel substitution. theme sheet absent, built-defaults used individual missing keys filled defaults, partial theme sheets supported. add_section(). New exported function adds new sheet existing curriculr workbook registers sections control sheet. new sheet pre-populated standard column spine including include_in_resume. sections row appended updated place without touching sheet’s formatting. -photo header layout. photo = NULL, CV.qmd now emits single full-width centered header block instead two-column grid empty left column.

title: curriculr 0.3.0
text: read_cv_data() reads theme sheet named character vector keyed key column, parallel profile sheet. Returns NULL sheet absent. read_cv_data() coerces section columns character import, including numeric year columns logical boolean columns, giving rendering pipeline uniform contract. read_cv_data() gains .coerce_col() internal helper normalizes NA strings produced .character(NA) back NA_character_. create_cv() injects variant use_icons CV.qmd Quarto params Quarto subprocess applies filtering icon settings calling R session. cv-data-template.xlsx template workbook gains theme sheet twelve default theming keys description column -workbook documentation. readme sheet updated document theme sheet reflect include_in_resume now present section sheets. Internal helpers .cv_theme_defaults(), .resolve_theme(), .build_format_block(), .build_typst_theme_block(), .fa_icon_map() added support theming icon assembly.

title: curriculr 0.3.0
text: openxlsx2 added Imports. readxl removed Imports.

title: curriculr 0.2.0
text: create_cv() redesigned. old scaffolding behavior replaced two distinct modes triggered whether data supplied .

title: curriculr 0.2.0
text: create_cv() scaffold mode (arguments) copies template workbook placeholder image current working directory prints step--step instructions. render. create_cv() render mode (data photo) reads workbook, writes CV.qmd, renders CV.pdf directory workbook. Added sections sheet support. workbook now controls sections rendered order. Row order render order. Deleting row excludes section. Adding row sheet follows standard column schema renders automatically. Added resolve_date_fun() maps date_fun token strings ("date", "year", "month_year", "year_only", "none") R date formatting functions. typst_escape(), cv_section(), resolve_date_fun() now exported. inst/templates/CV.qmd simplified – section rendering now driven iterating cv$sections. template uses sentinel strings create_cv() replaces resolved paths render time.

title: curriculr 0.2.0
text: read_cv_data() skips readme sheet entirely. read_cv_data() validates sections sheet column names read. read_cv_data() returns sections sheet row order without sorting. Non-ASCII characters cli messages replaced Unicode escapes CRAN portability.

title: curriculr 0.1.0
text: Initial release. read_cv_data() reads curriculr-formatted Excel workbook named list data frames. cv_render_section() renders CV section data frame raw Typst output inside Quarto document. create_cv() scaffolded new CV project template workbook, placeholder image, ready--render Quarto document.
